integration_inventory.name: IBMi Claims Adjudication API
integration_inventory.source_system: IBMi (AS/400) — RPG/CL programs on Db2 for i
integration_inventory.target_system: GCP Cloud Run (BFF layer) via Apigee X
integration_inventory.volume: Millions of real-time claims/day at POS; 99%+ electronically adjudicated
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: REST API via IBM IWS 3.0 → Apigee X gateway → Cloud Run BFF
integration_inventory.notes: Non-negotiable P95 ≤ 500ms latency. IBMi remains system of record during migration. IWS 3.0 (Jakarta EE) provides native REST exposure of RPG service programs with 20% performance improvement over IWS 2.6.

integration_inventory.name: IBMi Member Eligibility Service
integration_inventory.target_system: GCP Cloud Run BFF via Apigee X
integration_inventory.volume: High — lookup on every pharmacy transaction
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: REST API via IBM IWS 3.0 → Apigee X → Cloud Run BFF
integration_inventory.notes: First strangler fig migration candidate (lowest risk, highest visibility). Member eligibility is a read-heavy, stateless lookup — ideal for API extraction.

integration_inventory.name: IBMi Formulary Management Service
integration_inventory.target_system: GCP Cloud Run BFF via Apigee X
integration_inventory.volume: Moderate — formulary lookups per claim + clinical pharmacist updates
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: REST API via IBM IWS 3.0 → Apigee X → Cloud Run BFF
integration_inventory.notes: Clinical pharmacists manage formulary entries. Audit trail required for all formulary changes (clinical governance).

integration_inventory.name: IBMi Prior Authorization Service
integration_inventory.source_system: IBMi Db2 for i (PA tables + clinical notes)
integration_inventory.target_system: GCP Cloud Run BFF via Apigee X + Vertex AI pipeline
integration_inventory.volume: Moderate — subset of claims requiring PA review
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: REST API (IWS) + event-driven (Pub/Sub) for GenAI pipeline
integration_inventory.notes: GenAI demonstration workflow. PA submissions trigger Vertex AI pipeline for AI-assisted recommendation. Human-in-the-loop required for all PA decisions. Currently takes days for manual review — target minutes for standard cases.

integration_inventory.name: CDC: Db2 for i → Cloud SQL + BigQuery
integration_inventory.volume: Continuous — all database changes streamed via journal entries
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: Journal-based CDC → Precisely Connect or Striim → Pub/Sub → Dataflow → Cloud SQL + BigQuery
integration_inventory.notes: Google Datastream does NOT support Db2 for i — third-party CDC required. IBM i journals capture PT (insert), UP (update), DL (delete) entries. Must configure CHGJRNOBJ with *BOTH imaging for primary key updates. Precisely Connect reads native IBM i journals without triggers or table scans. Striim is Google Cloud partner with BigQuery reference architecture.

integration_inventory.name: myPBM Portal Integration (Azure)
integration_inventory.target_system: GCP Cloud Run BFF / IBMi shared backend
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: REST APIs over public internet (TLS 1.2+) or Azure-GCP cross-cloud VPN
integration_inventory.notes: Multi-cloud reality: myPBM on Azure, new architecture on GCP, IBMi on-premises. Visual consistency across platforms required (Phase 1 UX finding). myPBM already has 130+ REST APIs — integration is API-to-API, not legacy bridging.

integration_inventory.name: Health100 Platform Events (GCP)
integration_inventory.volume: High — event-driven, real-time for consumer engagement
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: Pub/Sub topics with schema registry + Eventarc for routing
integration_inventory.notes: Health100 is CVS's new health technology subsidiary (Google Cloud partnership, March 2026). Uses agentic AI with Gemini models. PBM events feed consumer engagement workflows. Schema registry enforces contract compatibility.

integration_inventory.name: Cloud Healthcare API (FHIR R4)
integration_inventory.source_system: Modernized PBM services / Db2 CDC stream
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: REST API (FHIR R4) with SMART on FHIR authorization
integration_inventory.notes: Supports CVS's interoperability goals and Health100 platform. FHIR R4 is the global standard for healthcare data exchange. Cloud Healthcare API is HIPAA-compliant with BAA. Enables data exchange across pharmacies, care providers, insurers.

integration_inventory.name: Vertex AI GenAI Pipeline
integration_inventory.volume: Moderate — PA submissions + clinical note processing
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: Vertex AI Pipelines (Kubeflow) for batch + online prediction endpoints for real-time
integration_inventory.notes: Healthcare NLP API deprecating May 2026 — must use Gemini/MedGemma-based extraction from start. MedGemma 1.5 (4B params, 128K context, 89.6% EHRQA accuracy). HIPAA-compliant with BAA, VPC-SC, CMEK. Human-in-the-loop required. Demonstrates Paul's GenAI DS team leadership capability.

integration_inventory.name: Enterprise Identity Federation
integration_inventory.notes: Federate existing enterprise IdP with GCP Cloud Identity Platform. IBMi user profiles must be mapped to cloud identities during transition. HIPAA-capable with BAA. WCAG 2.2 AA 3.3.8 requires accessible authentication (no CAPTCHAs).

integration_inventory.name: Apache Kafka → Pub/Sub Event Bridge
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: Kafka Connect Pub/Sub connector or Confluent Cloud GCP integration
integration_inventory.notes: CVS uses Apache Kafka for event streaming (per job postings). Bridge to Pub/Sub enables GCP-native event processing while maintaining existing Kafka producers. Kafka Connect has an official GCP Pub/Sub sink/source connector.

integration_inventory.name: Modern UI → BFF Layer
integration_inventory.integration_pattern: REST API + WebSocket/SSE for real-time features
integration_inventory.notes: BFF pattern: separate backends for web, mobile, and partner API clients. Each BFF aggregates data from multiple IBMi APIs optimized for its client. Split-pane views require parallel API loading. Command palette requires WebSocket or SSE for real-time fuzzy search. Performance budget: 200ms UI render + 300ms API response = 500ms total.

api_contracts.name: Member Eligibility Lookup
api_contracts.authentication: OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token (Cloud Identity Platform) + Apigee API key

success_200.copayStructure: object { generic: number, preferredBrand: number, nonPreferredBrand: number, specialty: number }

api_contracts.error_handling: Retry 3x with exponential backoff (100ms, 200ms, 400ms). Circuit breaker opens after 5 consecutive failures for 30s. Fallback: return cached eligibility from Cloud SQL (max staleness 15 min).

api_contracts.name: Claims Adjudication Submit
api_contracts.authentication: OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token + mTLS for service-to-service

success_200.rejectCodes: array of { code: string, description: string }
success_200.durAlerts: array of { severity: string, type: string, conflictingDrug: string, message: string }
success_200.pricingBreakdown: object { ingredientCost: number, dispensingFee: number, copay: number, planPay: number, patientPay: number }

error_422.details: array of { field: string, message: string }

api_contracts.error_handling: No retry for adjudication (idempotency risk). Circuit breaker: 10 failures in 60s opens for 60s. Fallback: route to legacy green screen path. Dual Run validation active during migration.

api_contracts.name: Formulary Search

query_params.query: string (drug name, NDC, or therapeutic class)

success_200.results: array of { ndc: string, drugName: string, genericName: string, tier: string, priorAuthRequired: boolean, stepTherapy: boolean, quantityLimit: object, therapeuticClass: string }
success_200.pagination: object { limit: integer, offset: integer, hasMore: boolean }

api_contracts.error_handling: Retry 3x with backoff. Fallback: return cached formulary from BigQuery (max staleness 24 hrs for formulary data).

api_contracts.name: Prior Authorization Submit and Status
api_contracts.authentication: OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token with clinical scope

body_POST.supportingDocuments: array of { type: string, content: string (base64) }

success_201_POST.status: string (SUBMITTED | IN_REVIEW | AI_RECOMMENDATION_PENDING | APPROVED | DENIED | APPEALED)
success_201_POST.aiRecommendation: object (nullable) { recommendation: string, confidence: number, citations: array of string, requiresHumanReview: boolean }

success_200_GET.reviewerDecision: object (nullable) { decision: string, reviewer: string, rationale: string, timestamp: string }
success_200_GET.auditTrail: array of { action: string, actor: string, timestamp: string }

api_contracts.error_handling: Retry 3x for GET. No retry for POST (idempotency via paId). PA submissions trigger Pub/Sub event for GenAI pipeline processing.

api_contracts.name: GenAI PA Recommendation (Vertex AI)
api_contracts.authentication: GCP IAM service account (internal only, not user-facing)

body.memberHistory: object { priorPAs: array, claimsHistory: array, diagnoses: array }
body.formularyContext: object { drugTier: string, alternatives: array, stepTherapyStatus: string }

success_200.citations: array of { source: string, relevantText: string }
success_200.alternativeDrugs: array of { ndc: string, name: string, tier: string, rationale: string }
success_200.evaluationScores: object { correctness: number, consistency: number, safety: number, groundedness: number }

api_contracts.error_handling: Retry 2x with 5s backoff. Fallback: route to manual review queue (no AI recommendation). Circuit breaker: 3 failures in 60s → skip AI for 5 min.

api_contracts.name: Health100 Event Publishing
api_contracts.notes: Protocol field uses 'Kafka' per schema enum constraint, but actual implementation uses GCP Pub/Sub — a managed event streaming service with Kafka-compatible semantics (publish/subscribe, topic-based routing, dead letter queues). Pub/Sub selected for native GCP integration with Health100 platform.
api_contracts.method: PUBLISH to Pub/Sub topics: claims.status, formulary.updates, member.notifications, pa.status
api_contracts.authentication: GCP IAM service account with pubsub.publisher role
api_contracts.error_handling: Pub/Sub handles retries natively. Dead letter queue after 5 delivery attempts. Schema registry validates event structure before publishing. 31-day message retention for event sourcing replay.

api_contracts.name: FHIR R4 Patient Resource
api_contracts.authentication: SMART on FHIR + OAuth 2.0 (patient/*.read scope for clinical data)

request_schema.fhir_r4_patient: Standard FHIR R4 Patient resource with extensions for PBM-specific fields

response_schema.success_201: FHIR R4 Patient resource JSON with server-assigned ID

api_contracts.rate_limit: Per Cloud Healthcare API quota (default 600 requests/min, increasable)
api_contracts.error_handling: Standard FHIR OperationOutcome error responses. Cloud Healthcare API handles concurrency via ETags.

api_contracts.name: CDC Change Stream (Db2 for i → GCP)
api_contracts.method: CDC journal stream via Precisely Connect or Striim
api_contracts.endpoint: Precisely Connect agent on IBMi → Kafka topic → Pub/Sub → Dataflow → Cloud SQL + BigQuery
api_contracts.authentication: IBM i system authentication (journal receiver access) + GCP service account

response_schema.target_cloud_sql: Transformed record in modern schema (UTF-8, ISO dates, normalized field names)
response_schema.target_bigquery: Append-only event record with metadata for analytics

api_contracts.rate_limit: Throughput-limited by network bandwidth (1-2 Gbps Partner Interconnect)
api_contracts.error_handling: CDC tool handles retry and exactly-once semantics. Dead letter for unparseable records. EBCDIC→UTF-8 conversion, packed decimal handling, date format normalization in Dataflow transformation.

api_contracts.name: User Authentication Flow
api_contracts.authentication: SAML 2.0 / OIDC federation with enterprise IdP

success_200.id_token: string (OIDC ID token with user claims and roles)

api_contracts.error_handling: Token refresh on 401. Session timeout after 30 min inactivity (HIPAA). MFA step-up for PHI access. Accessible auth flows per WCAG 2.2 AA 3.3.8 (no CAPTCHAs).

api_contracts.name: Observability and Audit API
api_contracts.method: Structured logging to Cloud Logging + Cloud Trace spans
api_contracts.endpoint: Cloud Logging API / Cloud Trace API (GCP-managed)

response_schema.success: Log entry persisted with 6-year retention for HIPAA

api_contracts.error_handling: Buffered writes with local fallback. Distributed tracing across IBM i API → BFF → Frontend request chain via OpenTelemetry.

data_flow_mappings.name: Member Data: IBMi → Cloud SQL
data_flow_mappings.source_format: EBCDIC fixed-length records, packed decimal, IBM i date formats
data_flow_mappings.target_format: UTF-8, ISO 8601 dates, standard numeric types

field_mappings.transform: SHA-256 hash (PHI — never store plaintext in cloud)
Code lookup: M→MALE, F→FEMALE, N→NON_BINARY, U→UNKNOWN
VARCHAR trim, format: XXXXX or XXXXX-XXXX

validation_rules: member_id must be non-null and unique
date_of_birth must be valid ISO 8601 date in the past
plan_id must exist in plans reference table
SSN hash must be exactly 64 characters (SHA-256)
status must be one of: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, TERMED

data_flow_mappings.name: Claims Data: IBMi → BigQuery
data_flow_mappings.source_format: EBCDIC records with NCPDP field identifiers
data_flow_mappings.target_format: BigQuery native types, STRUCT for nested objects

field_mappings.transform: Code lookup: P→PAID, R→REJECTED, D→PENDING, V→REVERSED
Delimited string → JSONB array of reject code objects
Delimited DUR alert records → JSONB array with severity, code, description
Code lookup: N→NOT_REQUIRED, R→REQUIRED, A→APPROVED, D→DENIED
CHAR(1) — DAW codes 0-9 per NCPDP standard

validation_rules: claim_id must be unique within partition
drug_ndc must be valid 11-digit NDC format
date_of_service must be within last 7 years (HIPAA retention)

data_flow_mappings.name: Clinical Notes: Unstructured → FHIR R4
data_flow_mappings.target_system: Cloud Healthcare API FHIR store + BigQuery (structured output)
data_flow_mappings.target_format: FHIR R4 DocumentReference + extracted structured entities

field_mappings.transform: Document AI OCR (for scanned) → UTF-8 text
Gemini entity extraction → ICD-10 code mapping
Vertex AI model output with confidence score

validation_rules: All extracted entities must have confidence score >= 0.8 or route to human review
ICD-10 codes must validate against current code set
PHI detected by Cloud DLP must be flagged in metadata
Original unstructured text preserved as DocumentReference for audit

migration_strategy.source_system: IBMi (AS/400) green screen applications — RPG/CL on Db2 for i
migration_strategy.target_system: GCP-native: React frontend → Cloud Run BFFs → Apigee X → IBM IWS → Db2 for i (with eventual migration of business logic to cloud-native services)

data_migration.approach: CDC-based continuous synchronization using Precisely Connect or Striim. Journal-based CDC reads native IBM i journals (PT/UP/DL entries) without triggers or table scans. Data flows through Pub/Sub → Dataflow (transformation) → Cloud SQL (transactional) + BigQuery (analytics). No big-bang data migration — data synchronization is continuous from day one.
data_migration.volume: Petabytes of pharmacy claims, member, formulary data accumulated over decades. CDC handles incremental changes; initial load via batch ETL for historical data.
data_migration.downtime_plan: Zero downtime. CDC operates continuously alongside production IBMi workloads. Initial batch load runs during maintenance window (Sundays 02:00-06:00 ET) for historical data backfill.

migration_strategy.timeline_note: Two timeline structures are used: (1) phased_approach (2 phases, 18 months total) describes the project delivery structure — what gets built and validated in each phase. (2) migration_sequence (4 phases, ~48 weeks) describes the workload migration order within the delivery structure — which IBMi workflows get migrated first. The migration_sequence phases execute within the phased_approach structure.
migration_strategy.rollback_plan: Each migration step is independently reversible. Apigee X routes traffic per-endpoint: flip routing from modern BFF back to legacy green screen path within minutes. Dual Run validates output equivalence before cutover — if validation fails, the legacy path remains primary. Feature toggles (Unleash, self-hosted for HIPAA) control per-user/per-role UI rollout. Rollback time target: < 30 minutes for any individual screen migration.

migration_sequence.name: Member Eligibility Lookup
migration_sequence.scope: First strangler fig candidate — read-only, stateless, high-visibility
migration_sequence.rationale: Lowest risk: no write operations, no transactional complexity. High value: demonstrates the pattern works end-to-end. Member eligibility is the most frequently called IBMi service.
migration_sequence.validation: Dual Run comparison of IBMi vs Cloud Run eligibility responses for 10,000 production queries. Zero discrepancy tolerance on member status and copay fields. Latency: P95 ≤ 200ms on new path.
migration_sequence.decision_gate: Dual Run passes 100% equivalence for 7 consecutive days

migration_sequence.name: Claims Adjudication
migration_sequence.scope: Highest-volume, most critical workflow — real-time at POS
migration_sequence.rationale: Highest business value but highest risk. Must maintain sub-second response times. Requires Dual Run for extended parallel validation period.
migration_sequence.validation: Extended Dual Run (30 days minimum). P95 ≤ 500ms. NCPDP B1 transaction parity. DUR alert equivalence. Pricing calculation accuracy to the penny.
migration_sequence.decision_gate: 30-day Dual Run with zero adjudication discrepancies and P95 ≤ 500ms

migration_sequence.name: Formulary Management + Prior Authorization
migration_sequence.scope: Clinical workflows including GenAI PA recommendation pipeline
migration_sequence.rationale: GenAI demonstration value. PA workflow is the flagship AI use case. Formulary management is tightly coupled to PA decisions.
migration_sequence.validation: Formulary data accuracy verified against IBMi source. PA workflow tested with clinical pharmacists. AI recommendation accuracy evaluated (correctness, safety, consistency). HITL approval rate tracked.
migration_sequence.decision_gate: Clinical pharmacist sign-off on PA workflow. AI recommendation accuracy ≥ 85% correctness.

migration_sequence.name: Remaining Workflows + Health100 Integration
migration_sequence.scope: Member management, reporting, analytics, Health100 event integration
migration_sequence.rationale: Lower-risk workflows that build on patterns established in phases 1-3. Health100 integration enables consumer engagement features.
migration_sequence.validation: All remaining green screen workflows functional in modern UI. Event publishing to Health100 validated. User adoption ≥ 80% (SC-004).
migration_sequence.decision_gate: All in-scope screens migrated. Green screen escape hatch usage < 5% of sessions.

cutover_criteria: Dual Run validates 100% functional equivalence for the migrating workflow
P95 latency meets or beats green screen performance
Zero data integrity issues in CDC synchronization for 14 consecutive days
User acceptance testing passed by representative pharmacy staff
Rollback tested and verified within 30-minute window
HIPAA compliance audit passed for the migrated component
Change management training completed for affected user groups

legacy_bridging.description: Anti-corruption layer translates between IBMi's physical file-based data model (fixed-length EBCDIC records, packed decimals, IBM date formats, single-character status codes) and the modern domain model (UTF-8, ISO 8601, enum types, nested objects). The ACL sits in the BFF layer on Cloud Run. Inbound: transforms modern API requests into IBMi-compatible format for IWS calls. Outbound: transforms IWS JSON responses into clean domain objects. This prevents legacy data conventions from leaking into the modern frontend and enables independent evolution of both sides.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Active throughout migration (18+ months). Gradually thins as business logic migrates from IBMi to cloud-native services. Not removed until a workflow is fully decoupled from IBMi.

legacy_bridging.legacy_system: IBMi green screen UI (5250 terminal emulation)
legacy_bridging.description: Green screen escape hatch: during transition, users can fall back to legacy 5250 terminal emulation via web-based terminal emulator (e.g., IBM ACS or Mochasoft). The modern UI shell provides a menu item to launch the escape hatch for any workflow. Usage is tracked as an adoption metric — high escape hatch usage signals migration issues. This addresses the 85% digital transformation failure rate driven by people/process issues.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Temporary — maintained until user adoption reaches ≥ 90% for each migrated workflow. Targeted for removal 6 months after Phase 4 completion.

legacy_bridging.description: Kafka-to-Pub/Sub bridge using Kafka Connect with the official GCP Pub/Sub sink connector. Existing Kafka producers continue publishing to Kafka topics. The connector mirrors events to Pub/Sub topics where GCP-native consumers (Cloud Run, Dataflow, Health100) process them. Enables gradual migration of event producers from Kafka to Pub/Sub without disrupting existing consumers.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Active until all event producers are migrated to Pub/Sub-native publishing. Estimated 12-18 months.

legacy_bridging.description: Apigee X serves as the unified API gateway for both IBMi-backed APIs and Azure myPBM APIs. Cross-cloud routing: Apigee routes PBM operational requests to IBMi (via IWS) or Cloud Run (migrated services), and client management requests to myPBM (Azure). This provides a single API surface for consumers regardless of backend platform, enabling transparent migration.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Permanent — Apigee X continues as the enterprise API gateway post-migration.

cicd_pipeline.overview: Cloud Build + Artifact Registry pipeline for containerized Cloud Run deployments. Separate pipelines for frontend (React micro-frontends via Module Federation) and backend (Cloud Run BFF services). IBMi RPG changes handled via existing CVS CI/CD (Jenkins/Git/Bitbucket).

1_code_quality.gate: Zero critical/high severity findings. Formatting consistent.

tools: Pact (contract testing between BFF and IBMi APIs)
2_testing.gate: Unit test coverage ≥ 80%. All contract tests pass. P95 latency within SLA under load.
2_testing.notes: Contract tests are critical: Pact validates that BFF expectations match actual IWS API responses. Prevents integration drift during migration.

3_security_scanning.gate: Zero critical CVEs. No secrets in code. OWASP Top 10 scan clean.

4_build.gate: Image builds successfully. Image size < 500MB. Semantic versioning applied.

5_deploy.strategy: Canary deployment via Cloud Run revision-based traffic splitting
5_deploy.progression: 5% → 25% → 50% → 100% with automated rollback on error rate > 1% or P95 latency degradation > 20%
tools: Cloud Run Release Manager (automated canary progression)
5_deploy.gate: Health check passes. Canary metrics within bounds. Smoke tests pass.

6_monitor.gate: Post-deploy validation: API health check returns 200. Error rate < 0.1% for 15 min. P95 latency stable.
6_monitor.alerts: PagerDuty/Opsgenie integration for P1 alerts (availability < 99.9%, P95 > SLA, error rate > 1%)

scheduled_automation: Weekly: Snyk dependency audit + automated PR for critical patches
Daily: Container image vulnerability scan against Artifact Registry
Monthly: Full OWASP ZAP scan of staging environment

phase_1.name: Foundation + First Strangler Fig (Months 1-6)
scope: GCP infrastructure setup (VPC, Cloud Interconnect, IAM foundation)
Partner Interconnect + HA VPN to IBMi data center (~$600-1,500/mo)
Apigee X deployment (Enterprise tier for HIPAA + HealthAPIx FHIR support)
IBM IWS 3.0 configuration for member eligibility RPG programs
Cloud Run BFF for member eligibility (first migrated workflow)
React shell application with Module Federation for micro-frontends
CDC pipeline setup: Precisely Connect or Striim → Pub/Sub → Cloud SQL + BigQuery
Cloud Identity Platform federation with CVS enterprise IdP
CI/CD pipeline (Cloud Build + Artifact Registry)
Design system foundation building on CVS Compass patterns + WCAG 2.2 AA
Dual Run validation for member eligibility
validation_criteria: Member eligibility API responds with P95 ≤ 200ms via Cloud Run BFF
Dual Run shows 100% equivalence with IBMi for member eligibility
CDC pipeline delivers changes with ≤ 5 second latency
CI/CD pipeline executes all 6 stages successfully
SSO works end-to-end (enterprise IdP → Cloud Identity Platform → React app)
HIPAA compliance audit passed for GCP infrastructure
phase_1.decision_gate: Go/no-go on expanding to claims adjudication. Validates IBMi integration pattern, CDC reliability, and frontend architecture.

phase_2.name: Claims Adjudication + GenAI Pipeline (Months 7-18)
scope: Claims adjudication API extraction via IWS (highest-volume IBMi API)
Claims adjudication BFF with NCPDP B1 transaction support
Extended Dual Run for claims (30-day validation minimum)
Formulary management API extraction
Prior authorization workflow with GenAI pipeline (Vertex AI + Gemini/MedGemma)
Clinical NLP pipeline: unstructured notes → structured FHIR resources
HITL review workflow for AI-generated PA recommendations
Health100 event integration via Pub/Sub
Cloud Healthcare API FHIR store for interoperability
Remaining workflow migrations (member management, reporting, analytics)
Full change management program: training, champion network, adoption metrics
Green screen escape hatch sunset planning
validation_criteria: Claims adjudication P95 ≤ 500ms through modernized path
30-day Dual Run with zero adjudication discrepancies
All clinical pharmacist personas can complete PA workflow in modern UI
Health100 events published and consumed successfully
User adoption ≥ 80% across all migrated workflows
Green screen escape hatch usage < 5% of sessions
phase_2.decision_gate: All in-scope workflows migrated. Legacy green screen sunset readiness assessment.

service_security.identity_model: Each Cloud Run service has a unique GCP service account. No shared service accounts. IAM roles follow least-privilege principle.

service_accounts.notes: Primary BFF for React frontend. Can invoke IBMi APIs via Apigee, publish events, access Cloud SQL.
Processes CDC events from Precisely/Striim. Transforms and loads to Cloud SQL + BigQuery.
Invokes Gemini/MedGemma models. Writes structured output to FHIR store. Logs evaluation metrics to BigQuery. No access to raw PHI storage — receives only de-identified clinical text.
Apigee X managed service identity. Routes to IBMi IWS and Cloud Run BFFs. Handles OAuth token validation, rate limiting, analytics.

service_security.secrets_management: Google Secret Manager with automatic rotation. API keys, database credentials, and IBM i connection strings stored as secrets. Accessed via workload identity — no secrets in environment variables or code.
service_security.audit_trail: Every service-to-service call logged: caller service account, target service, HTTP method, resource path, response code, latency, correlation ID. Logs shipped to Cloud Logging with 6-year retention (HIPAA). Cloud Audit Logs enabled for all admin and data access events.
service_security.network_security: VPC Service Controls perimeter around all HIPAA-regulated services. Private Google Access for Cloud Run. No public endpoints except Apigee-fronted APIs. HA VPN + Partner Interconnect for IBMi connectivity with IPsec encryption (<5ms overhead).

per_integration.ibmi_apis: 5 consecutive failures → OPEN for 30s. Critical: IBMi circuit breaker triggers green screen fallback notification.
per_integration.vertex_ai: 3 failures in 60s → OPEN for 5 min. Fallback: route PA to manual review queue (no AI recommendation).
per_integration.cdc_pipeline: Pipeline-level monitoring. Lag > 5 min triggers P2 alert. Lag > 30 min triggers P1 alert with manual investigation.

exceptions.claims_adjudication_POST: No retry (idempotency risk — duplicate claim submission)
exceptions.pa_submission_POST: No retry (idempotency enforced via paId deduplication)
exceptions.cdc_events: Infinite retry with backoff cap at 60s (data integrity requires delivery)

dead_letter_queue.implementation: Pub/Sub dead letter topics for each primary topic
dead_letter_queue.alerting: DLQ message count > 0 triggers P2 alert. DLQ message count > 100 triggers P1 alert.
dead_letter_queue.recovery: Manual review + replay via Cloud Functions triggered on DLQ subscription
